# Abstract
We analyze stress response in cultured fibroblasts using transcriptomic profiling. The study identifies pathways linked to mitochondrial regulation.

# Introduction
Cellular stress alters gene expression and metabolic balance. Understanding these shifts informs therapeutic strategies.

# Methods
Fibroblasts were cultured under oxidative stress for 24 hours. RNA sequencing and pathway enrichment were applied.

# Results
Differential expression highlighted mitochondrial transport and antioxidant pathways. Key regulators showed 2 to 3 fold changes.

Figure 1. Differential expression heatmap of stress response genes.

# Discussion
Mitochondrial regulation appears central to early stress adaptation. Follow up assays will test protein level changes.

Table 1. Top enriched pathways and scores.

# Conclusion
Transcriptomic profiling reveals coordinated stress pathways in fibroblasts. These findings guide targeted interventions.

# References
[1] K. Biologist. Oxidative stress transcriptomics. 2017.
[2] L. Biologist. Mitochondrial regulation review. 2019.
